from source import wrap

def test_wrap_trailing_text():
    text = "aaa bbb ccc"
    cols = 4
    expected = ["aaa", "bbb", "ccc"]
    result = wrap(text, cols)
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}"

import pytest
from source import wrap

def test_wrap_trailing_text():
    text = "aaa bbb ccc"
    cols = 4
    result = wrap(text, cols)
    expected = ["aaa", "bbb", "ccc"]
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected!r} but got {result!r}"

attempts.test_code:
import pytest
from source import wrap

def test_wrap_trailing_text_includes_last_word():
    """
    The wrap function should include all words in the output.
    The buggy implementation drops the final word(s) when the
    remaining text is shorter than the column width.
    """
    result = wrap("aaa bbb ccc", 4)
    expected = ["aaa", "bbb", "ccc"]
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}"
